description_long: This is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade that appears to be forged from dark metal. The blade features subtle color variations, including hints of reddish-brown and metallic gray, suggesting either heat treatment or decorative patina. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally from the base of the blade, providing hand protection. The handle is straight and cylindrical, ending in a small, rounded pommel, and appears to be made of the same dark metal as the blade. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements on the blade, guard, or handle, giving the sword a utilitarian and robust appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval European longswords, suitable for both display and practical use.
description: A long, straight, double-edged sword with a dark metallic blade, simple crossguard, and cylindrical handle. The design is utilitarian with subtle color variations and no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: The sword is seen from the side, showing the full length of the blade, crossguard, and handle, with color variations visible along the blade.
description_view_1: The sword is viewed edge-on, appearing as a thin vertical line, with the blade's thickness and straightness emphasized.
description_view_2: The opposite side view of the sword, similar to view 0, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle with visible color transitions.
description_view_3: The sword is again viewed edge-on from the opposite side, showing the thin profile and straight geometry.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical metal handle with rounded pommel, no visible wrapping or texture
